solution:
```
class Solution:
    def shortestMatchingSubstring(self, s: str, p: str) -> int:
        p = list(p)
        star1, star2 = p.index('*'), p.index('*', p.index('*') + 1)
        prefix, suffix = p[:star1], p[star2 + 1:]
        prefix_len, suffix_len = len(prefix), len(suffix)
        min_len = float('inf')
        for i in range(len(s) - prefix_len + 1):
            if s[i:i + prefix_len] == prefix:
                for j in range(i + prefix_len, len(s) - suffix_len + 1):
                    if s[j - suffix_len:j] == suffix:
                        min_len = min(min_len, j - i)
        return min_len if min_len != float('inf') else -1
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses two pointers to find the shortest matching substring in s that matches p. It first finds the prefix and suffix of p, then iterates through s to find the shortest matching substring.
